result:
Subject: Yesterday's payments outage — what happened and what we're doing

Hi there,

Yesterday, our payments service was degraded for approximately four hours, and some of you saw transactions fail outright. That's not the experience you trust us to deliver, and we're sorry. If your payment didn't go through, we understand the frustration — and the downstream impact on your own customers — and we take full responsibility.

The root cause was a misconfigured cache invalidation rule that, under a traffic spike, caused stale payment records to be served and new transactions to be rejected. Once identified, we corrected the rule, flushed the affected cache layer, and verified recovery end-to-end before declaring the incident resolved.

To prevent a recurrence, we're making two concrete changes. First, all cache and routing configuration changes will now go through canary rollouts, so a bad config is caught against a small slice of traffic before it reaches everyone. Second, we're expanding chaos testing in our staging environment to specifically simulate traffic spikes against cache invalidation paths, so failure modes like this one are surfaced before they ever reach production.

As a goodwill gesture, we're applying a credit to the accounts of every customer impacted during the incident window — no action needed on your end; you'll see it on your next statement. If you have questions or believe a failed transaction wasn't captured, please reply to this email and we'll make it right.

Thank you for your patience and for trusting us with your business.

— The Payments Team
